_template: ECA Rule Config — Event Condition Action
_description: Rules are stored as JSON config files in config/eca/. The ECA processor loads all rules, subscribes to their events, evaluates conditions, and executes actions. Everything is plugins.
label: Notify author when article is published

config.subject: Your article '[content:title]' has been published!
config.body: Your article is now live at [content:url].

actions._note: Tokens in [brackets] are replaced from the context stack

event_plugin.example: Event plugins hook into existing CMS hooks — content:create maps to the content service's create event

condition_plugin.example: Pure functions — receive context + config, return true/false

action_plugin.example: Side-effecting — send email, create content, fire webhook

token_system.resolver: Walk the context stack, resolve nested paths, return string

_implementation_notes.storage: config/eca/*.json — one file per rule, loaded at boot
